avoid: do not rewrite the full GEMM from scratch; cuBLAS is already best-in-class for the large matrix multiply
do not hardcode hidden dimensions or hidden tensors
do not allocate unnecessary intermediate tensors for the rank-16 path
evidence: speedup of 1.0 indicates identical performance to torch reference
correctness passes with zero error for all tested shapes
latency scales with d^3, matching expected GEMM behavior
profile summary consistently attributes bottleneck to multiple separate kernel launches
recommended_actions: keep cuBLAS for W @ X since it is already optimal
fuse the rank-16 update (B.T @ X and A @ result) into a single custom kernel to reduce launch overhead
evaluate precomputing Weff = W + A @ B.T and using a single Weff @ X GEMM, weighing extra allocation and outer-product cost against launch savings
test vectorized and unrolled versions of the rank-16 update to improve memory bandwidth and occupancy
